title: jailrun substitution manifest
description: Central contract artifact. probe(S2) produces it; bakery(S4) fills `native`; runtime(S1) consumes it. Decides, per binary in an OCI image, whether to run a native FreeBSD substitute or the Linux binary under Linuxulator.

generated_at.description: ISO8601; stamped by the caller, not the script

rootfs_snapshot.description: S3 snapshot_id the probe ran against

path.description: absolute path INSIDE the image rootfs

status.description: native = substitute with FreeBSD artifact; linuxulator = run Linux binary under ABI; missing = no way yet; unknown = not yet classified

native.description: How to satisfy natively. provider proposed by probe(S2); artifact_path resolved by bakery(S4).

artifact_path.description: resolved native binary path in the base (filled by bakery)

required.description: true if ANY binary stays status=linuxulator

_bakery.description: Internal metadata appended by bakery(S4).bake() — not part of the probe(S2)-produced contract proper, but a real, load-bearing extension: runtime(S1) reads _bakery.snapshot_id to bind-mount the registered native base into a run's clone. Declared here (added 2026-07-18) because additionalProperties:false previously rejected bake()'s own output against this schema.

snapshot_id.description: store.register_base() return value; null if the plan couldn't be auto-provisioned (e.g. it needs a build: recipe — see warnings)
